headline: EHS
Cyber
Security
Club
hero.subtitle: Learn ethical hacking, compete in CTFs, and build real-world security skills. Open to all students - no experience required.

about.heading: Why Join the EHS Cyber Club?
about.subheading: Cybersecurity is one of the fastest-growing fields in tech and you don't need to wait until college to get started. We're a student-led club that makes security skills accessible to everyone, regardless of background.
about.foundedText: Founded in 2026, EHS Cybersecurity Club has grown from a handful of students to one of the largest tech clubs in the school. We meet weekly to learn, practice, and build skills that matter.

pillars.title: Learn
pillars.desc: Hands-on workshops in ethical hacking, malware analysis, and digital forensics taught by students and professionals.

pillars.title: Compete
pillars.desc: Represent EHS in Capture The Flag competitions, CyberPatriot, and national cybersecurity championships.

pillars.title: Build
pillars.desc: Create real security tools, contribute to open source projects, and develop a portfolio that opens doors.

pillars.title: Connect
pillars.desc: Network with industry professionals, mentors, and a community of passionate students who share your interests.

items.title: Our Mission
items.text: To empower every student at EHS with cybersecurity knowledge and skills. Creating the next generation of defenders, builders, and leaders in digital security.

items.title: Our Vision
items.text: A future where every student graduates with the ability to protect themselves and others online. We believe security literacy is as important as reading and writing.

items.title: Our Values
items.text: Inclusivity, ethics, and curiosity above all. We celebrate diversity in thought and background. Hacking is about problem-solving, not breaking things.

cta.heading: Meetings every Thursday at 3:30 PM
cta.subtext: Room 632. Bring a laptop if you have one. No experience needed. Just curiosity.

westem.heading: Women in STEM
westem.subheading: WeSTEM works towards inspiring young girls to identify the flaws within the workplace in regards to gender inequality, by hearing the first-hand accounts of women in and pursuing STEM based fields.
missionParagraphs: Through this, we work towards encouraging members to act upon these inequalities in an attempt to resolve them. By holding this skill, members are better equipped to serve their communities by aiding in the creation of a safe space for individuals, no matter the differences they may hold.

WeSTEM hosts a series of workshops where members are taught technical skills such as coding and are exposed to logic-based problems associated with the different fields within STEM. Through this, members develop their critical thinking and problem solving skills as they are asked to directly apply them to the situation at hand and to think outside the box when faced with a problem.

features.title: Inspire
features.desc: Hear first-hand accounts from women in and pursuing STEM fields to understand and challenge gender inequality in the workplace.

features.title: Advocate
features.desc: Members are equipped to act on inequalities and help create safe, inclusive spaces for individuals of all backgrounds.

features.title: Build Skills
features.desc: Hands-on workshops teaching coding and logic-based problems drawn from real STEM fields.

features.title: Think Critically
features.desc: Develop problem solving skills by applying them directly to real situations and learning to think outside the box.

quotes.text: "WeSTEM showed me I belong in STEM. The conversations we have here are ones I couldn't find anywhere else."
quotes.name: Club Member

quotes.text: "The workshops pushed me to think differently about problems. I left every session with something new."
quotes.name: Club Member

join.heading: Ready to Join?
join.subheading: Membership is completely free. All skill levels welcome. Whether you're a complete beginner or an experienced hacker, there's a place for you here.

steps.title: Show Up
steps.desc: Come to any Thursday meeting in Room 214 at 3:00 PM. No sign-up required for first visit.

steps.title: Sign the Roster
steps.desc: Fill out our simple membership form. Takes 2 minutes. Gets you on the email list.

steps.title: Start Learning
steps.desc: Jump into workshops, join a CTF team, and connect with the community right away.

join.footerBadge: 100% FREE · NO EXPERIENCE NEEDED · ALL SKILL LEVELS WELCOME
